methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting phytoplankton, characterizing species, and maintaining cultures for experimental analysis.

steps.description: Monoclonal phytoplankton batch cultures were established by isolating single cells or chains of microalgae from seawater collected at the island of Helgoland.
The isolates were grown in 12-well plates with filter-sterilized artificial seawater medium (ESAW) containing essential trace metals and vitamins.
Pure and established cultures were transferred to 25 mL culture flasks and maintained at 15°C and a 12 h light/12 h dark diurnal cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer cultures to flasks and maintain under specific conditions

steps.description: Every week, aliquots of batch cultures were transferred into new medium at a concentration of 2000 cells mL−1 to maintain healthy and exponentially growing cultures.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer aliquots to new medium weekly

steps.description: Taxonomic identities were assigned by sequence similarity analysis of a fragment of the small and large subunit of the 18S and 28S ribosomal RNA gene.
steps.searchable_summary: perform sequence similarity analysis for taxonomic identification

methods.description: This method involves monitoring diatom growth using fluorescence measurements and analyzing photosystem II efficiency.

steps.description: Diatom growth was monitored daily in black 96-well polystyrene microplates by measurements of relative fluorescence units (RFU) of chlorophyll using a plate reader.
steps.searchable_summary: measure chlorophyll fluorescence to monitor diatom growth

steps.description: Diatom growth and performance were further monitored by pulsed-amplitude-modulation fluorometry by measurement of the minimal (F0) and maximal (Fm) dark fluorescence.
steps.searchable_summary: use PAM fluorometry to measure F0 and Fm

steps.description: The photosystem II (PSII) potential quantum yield (Fv/Fm) is the normalized ratio of F0 and Fm and represents the maximum potential quantum efficiency.

methods.description: This method outlines the steps for processing sequencing data, including trimming, merging, filtering, and clustering sequences for bacterial community analysis.

steps.description: Sequencing was performed in 2 × 300-bps paired-end-mode using the MiSeq Reagent Kit v3.
The trimmomatic package was used to crop the 300-275 bps and a sliding window length 3 allowed an average Phred quality score of 8 to filter from 5′ to 3′.
The paired-ends were merged with Vsearch with a minimum overlap of 40 bps and a maximum number of four mismatches.
Sequences were reverse complemented and both directions merged into one file.
The combined files were filtered for primer sequences, allowing 10% mismatch and a minimum overlap of 17 bps for the forward and 13 bps for the reverse primer.
Feature filtering to allow a maximum expected error per sequence of 1, a minimum length of 275 bps, a maximum length of 475 bps and a maximum number of ambiguities of 0.
Each sample was de-replicated independently and chimera-checked de novo.
All samples were pooled and dereplicated to produce a combined dataset as input for the swarm operational taxonomic unit (OTU) clustering method.
steps.searchable_summary: pool and dereplicate samples for OTU clustering

steps.description: Sequences were annotated with the default of the ribosomal database project classifier implemented in Mothur.
The taxonomy was assigned with the Silva v128 reference file prepared in Mothur at a confidence level of 80%.

methods.description: This method involves analyzing and comparing the community structures of bacterial inocula and established T. rotula_S16 bacterial consortia.

steps.description: Comparative sequence analyses of the 16S rRNA gene libraries revealed a total number of 5 million raw reads across all replicates after quality filtering.
steps.searchable_summary: perform comparative sequence analysis on 16S rRNA gene libraries

steps.description: The replicates of Experiment I were pooled prior to sequencing whereas the independent replicates of Experiment II were individually sequenced.
The individual bacterial inoculum communities differed significantly among each other, whereas all established T. rotula bacterial consortia were statistically the same.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze differences in bacterial community structures

steps.description: This was also reflected in the clustering pattern obtained by PCoA.
The established bacterial consortia were characterized by 27 OTUs responsible for a cumulative abundance of 94%.
steps.searchable_summary: characterize bacterial consortia by OTU abundance

steps.description: When individually comparing the abundances of 27 OTUs in the inocula and established T. rotula bacterial consortia, three cases could be distinguished.
steps.searchable_summary: compare OTU abundances between inocula and consortia
